original: Charles the Bald crowned king of Lotharingen
Leif Ericson reaches "Vinland" (possibly L'Anse aux Meadows, Canada), reputedly becoming the first European to reach North America
James I of Aragon conquers Valencia and founds the Kingdom of Valencia
Earliest mention of Prague's astronomical clock, the world's oldest still in operation, built by Mikuláš of Kadaň and Jan Šindel
Korean Hangul alphabet is first published by King Sejong the Great
Mérida is founded in Venezuela
General Fadrique Alvarez de Toledo and the Spanish disband the siege of Alkmaar
The Spanish army captures Cambrai
Eighty Years War: Maurice of Nassau, Prince of Orange with English support captures Breevoort, Netherlands from the Spanish
Peace of Pavia signed in Pavia between Spain and the Duchy of Savoy
Treaty of Khotyn is signed between the Ottoman Empire and Poland–Lithuania, recognizing Ottoman control over Moldavia and ending the Polish–Ottoman War
English parliament passes the Navigation Act meant to count the Dutch - requires English trade and fishing be carried on English ships
Swedish King Charles X Gustaaf occupies Kraków
Mass society storms palace of "heretics" Spanish governor Jose de Paternina
Collegiate School of Connecticut (Yale University) is chartered in New Haven
Great Northern War: Battle at Lesnaya (N.S): Russian army captures Swedish convoy
Britain & France sign treaty
Dutch governor of the East Indies Adriaan Valckenier allows the massacre of around 10,000 Chinese inhabitants of Batavia
Seven Years' War: Russian and Austrian forces occupy Berlin [OS=Sep 28]
Dutch merchant ship Vrouw Maria sinks off the coast of Finland
French troops occupy Hertogenbosch
Sinking of British frigate HMS Lutine, with the loss of 240 men and cargo worth £1,200,000 off Dutch coast
Hobart in Tasmania, Australia, founded
Prussia declares war on France
University of Gent officially opens
Congress of Aken returns to France from Libya
Guayaquil, Ecuador declares its freedom from Ecuador
Slavery is abolished in Costa Rica
Ioannis Kapodistrias, the first Head of State of modern Greece, is assassinated in Nafplion
The Royal College, Colombo, established with the name Hillstreet Academy in Sri Lanka
Meeting at the U.S. Naval Academy establishes the U.S. Naval Institute.
Steamboat "Home" sinks off Okracoke, North Carolina, killing 100
Eminent and controversial Anglican priest John Henry Newman leaves Anglican Curch of England and is received into the Roman Catholic Church
The siege of Sevastopol begins during the Crimean War
Joshua Stoddard of Worcester, Massachusetts, patents first calliope (steam powered musical instrument)
Battle of Brady Station, Virginia (Culpeper Court House, Bristoe Station)
First US underground pipeline for carrying oil is laid in Pennsylvania
Rome is incorporated into Italy by royal decree
World Postal Union forms in Bern Switzerland
1st 2-way telephone conversation, 1st over outdoor wires
American Humane Association organizes (Cleveland)
Washington Monument opens for public admittance
1st British troops reaches Durban, South Africa
South Africa President Kruger routes British authorities ultimatum
8.3 magnitude earthquake shakes Cape Yakataga, Alaska
11" (28.4 cm) rainfall in 24 hrs (NYC)
German troops take Antwerp in World War I
Belgrade, Serbia, surrenders to Central leaders
Gil Anderson races auto (165.1 km record) in Sheepshead Bay, New York
Louis Kaufmans comedy "Unchastened Woman" premieres in New York City

Baseball World Series: Cincinnati Reds beat Chicago White Sox 10-5 at Comiskey Park for a 5-3 series victory; due to the 'Black Sox Scandal', it is the last World Series to take place without a Commissioner of Baseball in place
Municipal Grant Park Stadium, in Chicago Illinois (now known as Soldier Field) is officially dedicated
Marcel Pagnol's play "Topaze" premieres in Paris
1st transcontinental flight by a woman completed, Laura Ingalls
Cleveland Rams and Chicago Bears play one of only 4 penalty free games in NFL history; Rams win 14-7 at Cleveland Stadium
Coup in Panama declares Ricardo Adolfo de la Guardia Arango the new president
Statute of Westminster 1931 passed by the Australian parliament, formalises Australian autonomy
Canadian offensive in West-Zeeuws-Flanders
German occupiers turn off electricity in Amsterdam
British troops occupy Andamanen in Gulf of Bengal
First electric blanket manufactured; sold for $39.50
First telephone conversation between occupants of a moving car and an airplane
Julie Styne and Sammy Cahn's musical "High Button Shoes" premieres at the New Century Theatre, NYC (later transferred to the Shubert, then to The Broadway Theatre, NYC; runs for 727 performances
WXYZ TV channel 7 in Detroit, MI (ABC) begins broadcasting
English ballerina Margot Fonteyn debuts in America with her performance in Tchaikovsky's "The Sleeping Beauty" at the Metropolitan Opera House in New York
United Nations Headquarters construction is completed, in New York City
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchillaproves Guyanese Constitution
KTIV TV channel 4 in Sioux City, IA (NBC) begins broadcasting
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Maralinga Australia
Israeli navy inaugrates its first submarine
Lee Harvey Oswaldarrives in Southampton, England
Cowboy QB Eddie LeBaron throws shortest touchdown pass (2")
Tanganyika, in east Africa, becomes independent within British Commonwealth
US members of communist party obliged to report themselves to police
Volcanic eruptions on Tristan de Cunha (South Atlantic)
Battles to decide Algeria-Morocco boundary kills 130
NASA civilian test pilot John B McKay takes X-15 to 39,200 m
Uganda becomes independent from the United Kingdom
French air force gets first nuclear weapons
Hurricane Flora ravages Cuba & Haiti, kills 6,000
Vajont Dam disaster, landslide creates a 50 million cubic meter wave, killing around 2,000 in the Piave Valley in Northern Italy
Beatles' "Yesterday" single goes #1 & stays #1 for 4 weeks
Government seizes oil fields in Peru
WKMU TV channel 21 in Murray, KY (PBS) begins broadcasting
Khmer Republic (Cambodia) declares independence
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
1st general striking in Luxembourg since 1942
Israel announces loss of Bar-Lev defense line in Suez Canal
Race riot in Boston due to "bussing"
Washington Capitals 1st NHL game, losing 6-3 to NY Rangers at Madison Square Garden; start of a 37 game road losing streak for Washington
Emperor Hirohitoof Japan visits San Francisco
Peter Petherick takes hat-trick on cricket debut New Zealand v Pakistan Javed 1st wkt
Test Cricket debut ofJaved Miandad(Pakistan), scores 163 on 1st day
Yanks 1st AL Championship game, beat Royals 4-1
Soyuz 25 launched to Saluyt 6, but returned after failing to dock
Yanks rally for 3 in 9th & beat Royals 5-3 for pennant #31

1st consumer use of home banking by computer by United American Bank in Knoxville, Tennessee
Nobel prize for literature awarded to Czesław Miłosz
Abolition of capital punishment in France.
Attack on synagogue in Rome leaves 1 dead
NFL Buffalo Bill QB Joe Ferguson passes for 419 yards with 5 TDs, winning 38-35, in overtime, over Dolphins, in Miami

Rangoon, Burma (now Yangon, Myanmar) bombing: North Korean agents attempt to assassinate South Korean President results in 21 deaths, including 9 South Korean government officials; presidential advisors and security team members; and 3 reporters
Astronaut Kathy Sullivan becomes 1st US woman to walk in space during a Space Shuttle Challenger mission (STS-41-G)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
US Senate convicts US District Judge Harry E Claiborne making him 5th federal official to be removed from office through impeachment; he had previously been criminally convicted of tax evasion
Japanese bank buys "Lady McGill" stamp for $1,100,000
17% vote for extreme-right Flemish Block in Belgium
Dennis Eckersley, 1st to save all 4 games in a championship series

Penthouse Magazine's Hebrew edition hits newsstands
David Souter, sworn in as 105th US Supreme Court Justice
Radio stations around world play "Imagine" honoringJohn Lennonon what would have been his 50th birthday
Ecuador becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
San Jose Sharks beat Calgary for their 1st NHL win, 4-3
A 13 kilogram (est.) meteorite lands in the driveway of Knapp residence, Peekskill, New York, destroying family's 1980 Chevrolet Malibu
Great meteorite seen from Kentucky to New York
Austrian parliamentary election (23% extreme-right)
ABL players allowed to own stock in the league
Hurricane kills 123 in Acapulco Mexico
Nobel prize for literature awarded to Italian playwright Dario Fo
North Carolina's record winning college basketball coach Dean Smith retires
NY Rangers are 1st NHL team to open with 4 straight ties
The last flight of the US aircraft Lockheed SR-71 "Blackbird"
Second mailing of anthrax letters from Trenton, New Jersey in the 2001 anthrax attack
North Korea conducts its first nuclear test with an estimated yield of between 0.4 and 2 kilotons
The Dow Jones Industrial Average closes at an all-time high of 14,164.53
“Low” single released by Flo Rida (Billboard Song of the Year 2008)
First lunar impact of the Centaur and LCROSS spacecrafts as part of NASA's Lunar Precursor Robotic Program
Serge Haroche and David Wineland win the 2012 Nobel Prize in Physics for work on quantum optics
60 people are killed in clashes between militias and Seleka rebels in the Central African Republic
Martin Karplus, Michael Levitt and Arieh Warshel win the 2013 Nobel Prize in chemistry for their work on multiscale models for complex chemical systems
Gatwick, Heathrow and JFK airports enhance screening for the Ebola virus
Patrick Modiano wins the 2014 Nobel Prize in Literature
The Tunisian National Dialogue Quartet is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize

Nearly 1 million people in northern California have their power cut by Pacific Gas and Electric to prevent wildfires amid high winds
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to John B. Goodenough, M. Stanley Whittingham and Akira Yoshino for development of lithium-ion batteries, Goodenough oldest-ever winner at 97
The UN's World Food Programme is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
American runner Emily Sisson runs US record 2:18:29 in Chicago Marathon, finishing 2nd by 4:11 to repeat winner Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya
Brightest cosmic explosion ever seen - a gamma ray burst 2.4 billion light years away, thought to be a massive star collapsing to form a black hole in a 1 in 10,000 year event
Claudia Goldin awarded the Nobel Prize in Economics “for having advanced our understanding of women’s labour market outcomes”
Robert F. Kennedy Jr. launches an independent bid for the US Presidency at Philadelphia’s Independence Mall
World record set for heaviest pumpkin, a jack-o’-lantern gourd weighing 2,749 pounds, grown by Travis Gienger from Anoka, Minnesota  - enough for 687 pies
World's largest offshore windfarm, Dogger Bank off the coast of Yorkshire, starts generating power for the UK electricity grid
Hurricane Milton makes landfall near Siesta Key, Florida, as a category 3 storm causing at least 23 deaths amid one of the state's largest-ever evacuations
Charlemagneand his brother Carloman I are both crowned King of the Franks
Richard Ileaves the Holy Land for England after initiating a three-year truce ending the third crusade
12-year-old French noblewoman Isabella of Angoulême is crowned Queen consort of England toKing Johnin Westminster Abbey
Last of 16,000 Jews expelled by KingEdward Ileave England
Religious dissidentRoger Williamsis banished from Massachusetts Bay Colony
Siege of Savannah (Georgia) during American Revolutionary War: Continental Army GeneralCasimir Pulaskiis wounded by grapeshot (a type of cluster munition), dies two days later
American inventorIsaac Singerpatents the sewing machine motor
Battle of Tom's Brook; Confederate cavalry that harassed Sheridan's campaign is beaten by GeneralGeorge A. Custer& Merrit's cavalry divisions
Nap Lajorie challengesTy Cobbbatting average with 8 hits, 6 were bunts as Brown's 3rd baseman Red Corriden played deep, Cobb still won
DutchQueen Wilhelminaopens Royal Colonial Institution
Baseball World Series: NY Yankees beat St. Louis Cardinals 7-3 at Sportsman's Park to become the first team to sweep consecutive World Series;Babe Ruthsmashes 3 HRs for the Yanks

Part of the lost Piri Reis World Map is rediscovered in the Topkapi Palace Library in Istanbul. Compiled by Ottoman general Piri Reis in 1513, it is the earliest known map of the voyages ofChristopher Columbus
Soviet leaderJoseph Stalinexpels Lev Kamenev and Grigory Zinoviev from the Communist Party after a power struggle

Saddam Husseinthreatens to hit Israel with a new missile

Action thriller film "Under Siege" directed by Andrew Davis starringSteven Seagalin his most famous role is released
